what_ethan_runs_do: Current closure/thermal setup evidence should use Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline continuation rows. Salt 1 remains held pending newer qualification, and corrected Salt perturbations remain status-only until admitted by a formal gate.
quantitative_evidence: Salt 2/3/4 are fit-admissible current mainline in the scenario contract; Salt 1 rows are held; corrected Salt rows are held_until_formal_gate.
interpretation_or_risk: Do not mix admitted mainline evidence with Salt 1 or corrected-Q rows when documenting nominal setup behavior.

what_ethan_runs_do: Readable Salt CFD case boundary dictionaries contain a 0.03556 m layer, labeled as the 1.4 in wall/insulation layer. The 0.25 in and 0.30 in values in older friction/temperature sweeps are 1D diagnostic settings, not the CFD case label.
quantitative_evidence: unique_thickness_m includes 0.03556 for Salt 1/2/3/4 mainline and corrected rows; has_1p4in_insulation_layer=True for mainline rows.
interpretation_or_risk: The CFD setup should be described as 1.4 in layer present unless a deeper boundary-condition implementation audit redefines the physical meaning of that layer.

what_ethan_runs_do: Mainline Salt case 0/T files contain externalTemperature, rcExternalTemperature, and zeroGradient boundary types. Heater, passive walls, and test section are carried mostly through rcExternalTemperature; cooler patches are externalTemperature; junctions are mixed.
quantitative_evidence: Mainline rows report bc_type_counts_json = externalTemperature:10, rcExternalTemperature:36, zeroGradient:23.
interpretation_or_risk: Any setup summary should discuss patch role and boundary-condition family together. A single phrase such as 'fixed temperature' or 'radiation boundary' is too coarse.

what_ethan_runs_do: rcExternalTemperature patches carry emissivity metadata, but audited case roots have no constant/radiationProperties and no qr or G field. Patchwise heat ledgers therefore set radiation_present=False and preserve emissivity as metadata only.
quantitative_evidence: rc_external_patch_count=36, emissivity_patch_count=36, has_radiation_properties=False, has_qr_or_G_field=False for mainline rows.
interpretation_or_risk: Do not add a separate CFD radiation correction on top of wallHeatFlux. 1D radiation_on is a forward-model capability, not evidence of a separate CFD qr ledger term.

what_ethan_runs_do: CFD heater patches are intended heater input, but the realized fluid-side heater wallHeatFlux is lower than imposed heater duty. CFD-informed comparisons should use heater wallHeatFlux into the fluid, not resistor/imposed electrical wattage.
interpretation_or_risk: The gap is a boundary/solid/storage/staging caveat until a same-time solid-energy audit exists. It should not be hidden as a tunable closure coefficient.

what_ethan_runs_do: Current Fluid salt cases include a separate 37 W quartz/test-section source, but the CFD test-section wallHeatFlux is a net sink in the July 8 patchwise ledger.
quantitative_evidence: CFD test-section wallHeatFlux W: Salt 2 -5.680, Salt 3 -10.545, Salt 4 -16.769. Fluid cases.yaml salt rows use test_section_power_W=37.0.
interpretation_or_risk: The current 1D source contract is not equivalent to the CFD wall-flux observation. Treat test-section power as a setup mismatch and preserve the sign convention.

what_ethan_runs_do: CFD cooler specified duty and cooler wallHeatFlux align closely. The current 1D predictive air-side HX removes far less heat than the CFD cooler patch group.
quantitative_evidence: CFD cooler removal W: Salt 2 136.351, Salt 3 150.770, Salt 4 169.227. Fixed-mdot current 1D qhx W: Salt 2 46.292, Salt 3 49.663, Salt 4 53.472.
interpretation_or_risk: The cooler/HX path is the largest current thermal-state lever; prescribing CFD cooler duty drops fixed-mdot mean-T error from about 63.75 K to 4.46 K mean across Salt 2/3/4.

what_ethan_runs_do: Passive ambient walls are grouped as ambient_wall with rcExternalTemperature. The resolved thermal network uses wallHeatFlux, wall/bulk temperature support, thicknessLayers/kappaLayerCoeffs, boundary h, and ambient temperature where available.
quantitative_evidence: CFD ambient_wall wallHeatFlux sums W: Salt 2 -62.234, Salt 3 -68.320, Salt 4 -75.927. Boundary ambient T K: 299.19, 299.79, 299.97. Boundary h range W/m2K across resolved rows: about 3.424-10.060.
interpretation_or_risk: Passive losses are validation diagnostics until per-segment reconstruction is complete. Do not treat a global 1D insulation scalar as a paper-grade reconstruction of the CFD wall stack.

what_ethan_runs_do: Junction and stub/extension patches are grouped as junction_other diagnostic wall flux. They are not bracketed by endpoint-temperature spans.
quantitative_evidence: CFD junction_other wallHeatFlux W: Salt 2 -39.128, Salt 3 -43.235, Salt 4 -48.485. Quality flag includes junction_grouped_mixed_bc.
interpretation_or_risk: Junction heat is real in the ledger but not yet a clean segment closure target. Keep it grouped and diagnostic until raw bracketing improves.

what_ethan_runs_do: The defended July 1 local 1D setup is Fluid v2, predictive_airside_hx, baseline 1.0 in insulation, radiation_on=true, heater power plus 37 W salt test-section source, and predictive cooler duty from air-side boundary conditions.
quantitative_evidence: Defended scenario: ethan_cfd_informed_salt_baseline_ins_1.0in_rad_1. It is not a published global 1.4 in Salt scenario. Baseline mean energy error was 11.27% heater and mean mass-flow error 26.69% against the frozen surface.
interpretation_or_risk: Do not call the July 1 defended 1D scenario physically matched to the CFD 1.4 in boundary label. It is a bounded surrogate until a matching 1.4 in or reconstructed boundary scenario is run.

what_ethan_runs_do: The fixed-mdot replay holds mdot to CFD, solves only thermal periodicity, and reports pressure residual diagnostically. Baseline P0 keeps current Fluid thermal contract; P1 prescribes CFD cooler duty only; P4 prescribes CFD cooler duty plus CFD heater wall flux.
quantitative_evidence: P0 mean |Tmean error| 63.75 K. P1 mean |Tmean error| 4.46 K. P4 mean |Tmean error| 39.75 K and over-cools the loop.
interpretation_or_risk: Fixed-mdot rows diagnose thermal boundary sensitivity; they are not predictive hydraulic solutions and should not be scored as mdot predictions.

what_ethan_runs_do: Current documentation is thorough but distributed. A paper-grade setup claim still needs a normalized boundary contract, fixed-mdot/reconstructed thermal replay gate, no-qr radiation policy, and mesh/GCI/time-window uncertainty carried with the setup table.
quantitative_evidence: Thermal boundary contract requires abs(Tmean error)<=2 K and abs(loop delta T error)<=1 K for replay gate; current P1 still misses strict 2 K mean-T gate.
interpretation_or_risk: Use this reference as the setup map, not as final validation. Final claims require replay under a declared latest-window and uncertainty contract.
